The Neoclouds have started running ($NBIS $IREN $CRWV)

Cooling those chips is a big deal. $INV $VRT $JCI

I think cooling is "just warming up".

text:
PO, CPO를 포함한 Photonics 섹터에서
PIC 설계, 제조사들은 200G/lane이상을 이야기하며 그 이상을 바라보지만

제대로된 EIC 200G/lane을 설계하고
그걸 Foundry로 만들 수 있는 기업은 한정적인데 사람들은 그걸 간과하고 있는듯하다.

Photonics는 PIC, EIC, DSP, LASER, PD Detector, 차세대 패키징이 한번에 가능해야 비로소 제대로 만들어졌다 할 수 있다.

PIC 200G를 시연에 성공했어도, PD Detector가 그걸 못받아 들이는 사양이면 무용지물이고, EIC가 신호를 제대로 못주면 그것 또한 의미 없는 것이다.

그 신호를 밀어줄 EIC/driver, 받아줄 PD/TIA, 보정할 DSP, 버틸 laser, 그리고 그걸 낮은 loss와 높은 yield로 묶는 패키징이 같이 맞아야 한다.

결국 Photonics는 부품 기술이 아니라, 시스템 통합 싸움이다.

text:
$AMD just reported a monster ER and sent every CPU stock to the moon. Up +15% after hours.

Since I started publicly sharing my portfolio I’ve been clear about where I’m putting my money and why:

The AI buildout: 

Photonics / Memory / CPUs / NeoClouds

So why CPUs and why now?

$AMD said it best on the call: in the past the CPU-to-GPU ratio was 1-to-4 or 1-to-8. Now it’s moving toward 1-to-1. And with agentic AI, you could end up with more CPUs than GPUs. That’s a structural shift.

Here’s how my CPU basket is doing since I entered:

$INTC (January) – entry at ~$55, bought the local top because I’m the worst trader ever. Now +105%.

$AMD (late March) – entry at ~$202, bought the local bottom because I’m the best trader ever. Now +76%.

$ARM (late March) –entry at $137 after the CPU for AI thesis started gaining traction. Now +62%.

If you think you’re late I can assure you you’re not. Not super early either. But agentic 

AI spending is just getting started and CPUs are in the critical path of every dollar spent.

Still bullish all CPU plays.

In a new blog, Joseph Chon explains why integrating MACsec directly into coherent DSPs inside ZR/ZR+ pluggable modules represents a meaningful shift in how scale-across networks are secured. Compared to embedding MACsec in switch ASICs or placing dedicated security devices on circuit boards, module-based MACsec reduces cost, complexity and deployment risk while making upgrades significantly easier. Marvell has already introduced several industry firsts enabling this approach, including the Electra 2nm 1.6T coherent DSP, the Libra 800G coherent DSP and the Aquila M coherent-lite DSP, all with integrated MACsec. By 2030, pluggables are projected to account for approximately 85% of scale-across ports shipped. text:
Threadguy explains why he slammed Zcash this weekend 

“Everybody in the stock market is so rich right now. Everybody is up so much money… there is just trillions of dollars that have been printed in the stock market”

“They’re ripping everything. We’re doing photonics now, everything’s an AI bottleneck… anything that could be remotely related to AI that hasn’t pumped, rip that, South Korea stocks now… rip that”

“When I saw the metals to full AI to South Korean stock rotation I’m like people are desperate for some reflexivity”

“There’s three assets in crypto that just haven’t moved Hyperliquid, Zcash and Bitcoin. Zcash is like an $8B coin… it’s worth nothing”

“I’m watching Google add $500B in a day and I’m saying to myself man they’re gonna pop it”

“Bitcoin, Zcash and Hyperliquid all of a sudden look good. I was bidding Zcash a little bit but then I just slammed over the weekend”

“Bitcoin, Zcash, Hyperliquid are the three set up… I just got the tingle”

Photonics and memory are both getting re-rated right now because AI infrastructure got declared a national security priority.

Optimistically you’re looking at 40-60% CAGR on photonics through 2028 with 

CPO as the accelerant. Memory, specifically HBM, is already running 50%+ growth and that probably holds or steepens as cluster density increases.

The Goldman re-rate just gave institutional cover for what was already obvious to anyone close to the hardware stack.

Everyone is looking for the next Nvidia.

The real money is in what Nvidia depends on.

Bookmark.

• Photonics → $LITE $COHR $AAOI
• Networking → $MRVL $ANET  
• Memory → $MU $RMBS  
• Power → $VICR $NVTS $MPWR  
• Grid → $VRT $ETN $PWR  
• Foundry → $TSM $AMKR  
• Inference → $ARM $AMD $INTC  

Many already partner with Nvidia directly.

Nvidia scales only if this stack scales.

That’s where the asymmetry is.

Follow the right people on X.

text:
$SITM my second blockbuster this season, after $STRL.
25% up on earnings!!!

I was a bit nervous because of the cost of Renesas acquisition in Feb. 

All the photonics chasers in X miss out this gem. I don't know how and why.

High precision clock is very important for synchronization in any digital communication -- between logic units (GPUs, CPUs, TPUs) for parallel computation and conversion of electrical signals to optical signals. That's right. There is a unit called DSP (digital signal processor) which takes binary electrical data from the logic units and transforms it into an electrical signal for light modulation unit which chops the CW light. For 800G/1.6T to work, it requires to handle sub-70 femtosecond jitter. Although $SWKS can achieve this, but in a convoluted way. $SITM's MEMS Resonators are tiny and work solid at 100C. Ideal for optical tranceivers and CPOs.

Another huge positive story is Renesas integration, where $SITM can vertically integrate this crucial part of the photonic fabrics. Instead of having a separate clock chip on a board, Renesas' microcontroller unit can have $SITM's clock integrated inside the package. With this, $SITM will be able to distribute the timing signal, clean jitters for 800G/1.6T networking, synchronize 5G/6G base stations. It will be unbeatable!
embedded_text:
$SITM is creating a very wide moat around high speed MEMS, I think.

EpiSeals, DualMEMS, plua 100+ patents owned by Luts
Now, adding all IPs from Renesas

Laser, Photonics, ... without ultrafast clocking doesn't make much sense for sure.

Wicked all the way down to the 9EMA and the ATH AVWAP and bounced hard, showing strong signs that the uptrend is still very much intact.

Now we have to reclaim that important $15.24 level, and then we can start talking about $19…

text:
$IBKR – here’s how to get real-time market data for any international exchange on IBKR

After going deep on photonics bottleneck plays outside the U.S. (thanks Serenity), I kept running into the same problem: no real-time data. Japan, Taiwan, UK, France, Germany, Sweden ($SIVE right?) – all delayed or missing.

You have two options: buy at market price blind, or pay $1-2/month per exchange for live data.

I went with the latter. Here’s how to set it up.

Step-by-step guide:

1.Log into IBKR and go to Settings (top right, your profile icon)
https://t.co/wi6vysjrJb the Settings menu, tap Market Data Subscriptions
https://t.co/ieOXkA2beU’ll see your current subscriptions listed by region – North America, Europe, Asia Pacific
4.Tap Configure (the gear icon in the top right of the Subscriptions panel)
https://t.co/7lNGUKtQOx for the exchange you want – Taiwan Stock Exchange, Japan TSE, LSE, etc.
6.Add it. Most non-U.S. exchanges run $1-2 USD/month.
7.Confirm and you’ll get a “Thank You” screen once the subscription is active

Is it worth it? Depends on your position size.

If real-time data helps you save just 1% on entry, that’s $100-$1,000 back in your pocket depending on how much you’re deploying – which could be several years worth of the monthly fee in a single trade.

For me, it’s a no-brainer.

I went with the latter. Here’s how to set it up. Step-by-step guide: 1.Log into IBKR and go to Settings (top right, your profile icon) https://t.co/wi6vysjrJb the Settings menu, tap Market Data Subscriptions https://t.co/ieOXkA2beU’ll see your current subscriptions listed by region – North America, Europe, Asia Pacific 4.Tap Configure (the gear icon in the top right of the Subscriptions panel) https://t.co/7lNGUKtQOx for the exchange you want – Taiwan Stock Exchange, Japan TSE, LSE, etc. 6.Add it. Most non-U.S. exchanges run $1-2 USD/month. 7.Confirm and you’ll get a “Thank You” screen once the subscription is active Is it worth it? Depends on your position size. If real-time data helps you save just 1% on entry, that’s $100-$1,000 back in your pocket depending on how much you’re deploying – which could be several years worth of the monthly fee in a single trade. text:
RARE EARTH THEME: Setting up Huge 

Rare earth elements are the "vitamins" of the 2026 technology buildout. You only need a small amount, but without them, the high-performance hardware required for AI, photonics, and space infrastructure simply won't function.

They are the PHSYICAL BOTTLENECK for AI, As of 2026, China still refines over 90% of the world's supply. This has created a "stockpiling" environment as the U.S. and E.U. rush to bring non-Chinese refining facilities (like MP Materials in California) online before new defense bans take effect in 2027.

The Time Gap: While a data center can be built in months, opening a new mine and refinery often takes a decade. This mismatch between skyrocketing demand and slow supply growth is the primary reason for the "rerate" we're about to see. 

When a entire group is setting up, you pay attention.

text:
LWLG — a real materials bet wrapped in a momentum stock NFA

Lightwave Logic makes Perkinamine electro-optic polymers that spin-coat onto silicon photonics wafers in a back-end-of-line step  (<100°C) to convert passive silicon waveguides into sub-volt, high-bandwidth Mach-Zehnder modulators. 

The technology’s figure of merit (Vπ·L ≈ 0.1 V·cm) is roughly an order of magnitude better than silicon plasma-dispersion modulators and competitive with thin-film lithium niobate. 

The relevance to AI is direct: 800G/1.6T/3.2T optical interconnects and co-packaged optics need exactly this — low-drive-voltage, 

BEOL-compatible, foundry-integrable modulators.
Commercial reality is far less mature. FY2025 revenue: $237 thousand   (yes, thousand) on a $2.45B market cap (P/S ~10,000x). Net loss $20.3M;  cash $69M  after  the December 2025 $35M offering at $3.10/share.  Management explicitly states no meaningful production revenue until 2027.   

The investor must accept this is a binary 2027 outcome. The bull case rests on GlobalFoundries PDK integration (March 2026), Tower Semiconductor tape-out, SilTerra/Luceda partnership, and four 

Fortune Global 500 customers in Stage 3 of the Design Win Cycle. Importantly, the Marvell-Polariton acquisition (closed 
February 2026,  ~$3.25B + $2.25B earnout)  creates a tantalizing but unconfirmed technical link — Polariton’s plasmonic-organic-hybrid demos historically used Perkinamine,  but Marvell has announced no LWLG license or supply agreement.  

Bulls are extrapolating; bears point out the company has been “near commercialization” since its PSI-TEC days in the 2000s.

text:
La @utec_tgo continúa fortaleciendo su presencia internacional en el ámbito científico y tecnológico con el nombramiento de la Dra. Ana Karen Reyes como Vicepresidenta Asociada de Vinculación Global con Miembros de la IEEE Photonics Society para el periodo 2026–2028, uno de los organismos más importantes a nivel mundial en investigación y desarrollo tecnológico. 🌎🔬

Este reconocimiento refleja la calidad académica y de investigación que impulsa la UTEC, fortaleciendo la generación de conocimiento, la innovación y la vinculación con comunidades científicas internacionales, en concordancia con la visión educativa promovida por el gobernador @juliomenchaca_ y el secretario de Educación Pública de Hidalgo, @natycastrejonv 📚✨

DARPA also moved AMPED from concept to competition, with advanced photonics bids due August 9, 2026. The program targets photonic-crystal devices for sensing, communications, and electronic warfare. That is early visibility into which labs can become programs.

text:
"15 minutes of exposure to this can reduce blood glucose elevation by 27.7%."
— Journal of Biophotonics, 2024

"It activates the enzyme inside your cells that produces ATP, the energy currency of every living cell."
— Frontiers in Photonics, 2024

"It improves muscle recovery, accelerates wound healing, and supports tissue repair across dermatology, neurological function, and metabolic health."
— News Medical / Photobiomodulation review, 2026

All refer to red light.

The same wavelengths your great-grandparents got at sunrise and sunset every day.

The same wavelengths almost completely missing from modern indoor life.

Combined value: ~8 billion SEK. https://t.co/k9EMCinhs5 It is framed as these investors are driving this on speculation. Yes maybe, that is of course possible. Or is there another angle? My take, SEK8B..what? Hence my reaction would probably be the opposite, this could be worth investigating: What thesis are these allocators actually trading? Is it pure speculation with SEK 8B in play? Who sits on these SEK8B? EFN however have asked "Richard Schatz, senior researcher in photonics at RISE Research Institutes of Sweden and former consultant for Sivers", for his opinion about Sivers. So what did Mr. Richard Schatz say: "There are several companies that manufacture these kinds of lasers, and Sivers is far from alone. I don't understand why the company has become so hyped. There are many players, not just in Europe and the US, but also in China," says Schatz. Honestly I have been COO and CEO at Sivers for almost 10 years 2015-2024 (2015 we had 18 headcount I known everyone in the Company until 2024) and also ask Board members that was in the board many years before me, both for Sivers and former CST Global (Sivers Photonics today) and none of them can recollect Mr. Schatz. I also sent an email, asking the reporter that interviewed Mr S and asked when he claims to have been a consultant at Sivers. Without reply. This doesn't really matter, it is just shows where the digging stops. Sivers Semiconductors is one of a handful of merchant InP laser suppliers globally. They have disclosed partnerships with POET Technologies, Jabil (1.6T LRO), O-Net, Ayar Labs and operate in the External Light Source category that scale-up CPO architectures depend on. Either are all these US investors dramatically wrong about the photonics layer of AI infrastructure, or the institutional allocators routing through US brokerages have identified what most Swedish investors haven't yet. Maybe this is worth investigating which? To be 100% clear no one knows (not even me), who has the right hypothesis, that we might know in 2028-2030 time frame. To help anyone intressted, I made this free and open substack to explain how the money flows. As they said in Washington follow the money... text:
A great feature by MIT on Piris Lab, a Tekedia Capital portfolio company developing photonics systems to power the next-generation infrastructure of AI.

Largely, Piris Labs is building a full-stack AI inference platform designed to eliminate one of the most critical constraints in modern computing: the data movement bottleneck. By combining proprietary photonic hardware with a vertically integrated software stack, the company addresses the “memory wall” that limits the efficiency of today’s GPU-based systems.

Read here  https://t.co/0yykNVI8eL

Their approach delivers comparable performance to traditional compute clusters, at a significantly lower cost, by improving effective FLOP utilization and reducing latency. In doing so, Piris Labs is helping make the unit economics of trillion-parameter AI models truly sustainable.

Tekedia Capital >> we fund innovations

text:
The 800G to 1.6T transition is mid-ramp now, accelerating into 2026 alongside Rubin.

Test and validation aren't optional. Every new module needs production qualification.

If silicon photonics is the wave, $VIAV is the toll booth most AI portfolios are missing.

$VIAV: $12B cap, 8.8x sales, P/E 40.

Pure-play photonics names like $LITE and $COHR trade richer because the market prices them as direct beneficiaries.

VIAV gets paid by every transceiver vendor regardless of which one wins the 1.6T design slot.

Silicon photonics is the chokepoint Nvidia's Rubin platform is built around. Copper is out, light is in across every AI server.

Inside that basket, $VIAV sits at the test layer. Every 1.6T transceiver shipped gets validated on their gear before production. https://t.co/ExNUXvx0mr

text:
My exposure to photonics 

*You've basically called all of them

Think these are positioned well to capture capex ramp over the next 3-5 years 

SIVE 
- Sivers Semiconductors (lasers for CPO optical engines)

OE Solutions  
- InP lasers + ELSFP modules for CPO

LPK (LPKF)    
- Laser systems for glass interposers & advanced photonics packaging

ALMU          
- Aeluma (compound semiconductor photonics / quantum dot lasers)

Episil Precision (https://t.co/TuTwLjmIHE) 
- Silicon photonics epi wafers

IQE 
- Compound semi epi wafers (InP/GaAs/GaN) for photonics

SOI (Soitec)  
- Engineered substrates (SOI) critical for silicon photonics

ShunShin (https://t.co/f1ZwK4ljHn) 
- Optical packaging & assembly for CPO

text:
$inv
Reposting again 

Everyone still has eyes on the memory and photonics  trade $SNDK $MU $DRAM $LITE $COHR $SIVE

Whilst there might be some more room in these still, they are becoming somewhat crowded. 

Exactly why Goldman is saying liquid cooling is the next trade like memory. 

Liquid cooling has lagged the overall data centre trade.

All the private cooling companies are getting hoovered up at the moment in big private deals. $ETN and Boyd. $ECL and Cool IT. $VRT and Strategic Thermal Labs. 
These big companies are trying to build out their end to end solutions for next gen cooling. 

Then you’ve got the Series B round of $jci in @AccelsiusATX $inv

It’s actually hard to get exposure to a public pure play cooling company right now. 
I think $inv is the closest way with there 43.2% holding in @AccelsiusATX 

From my research it’s abundantly clear the direction liquid cooling is going.

$inv
From the GS global equites call this AM

They expect liquid cooling to be the next bottle neck. Similar to memory last year $mu $sndk $5930.sk SK Hynix

A great example of this was: the memory theme that emerged late last year. The current bottlenecks now exist in fiber https://t.co/B6Ws7wS5lD

text:
Few more thoughts on $SIVE 

InP laser chokepoint + RF‑photonics flagship, with Glasgow’s constrained InP capacity, a SEK 304M FY25 revenue base, positive Q4 photonics EBITDA, and a $453M pipeline giving it four distinct growth vectors (LiDAR, CPO ELS, SATCOM/wireless, and telecom/defense/6G) where any single one could eventually rival today’s total business.

Still upside from here, we've got a MASSIVE research report coming Saturday on $SIVE.

A unique look that not many are exploring.

Will send here: https://t.co/lUESQXjyp9

text:
$AEVA is no longer just a LiDAR story.

The market is still valuing it like a speculative auto sensor company while management is quietly positioning for the next AI infrastructure wave:

• Silicon photonics
• Optical interconnects
• AI data center bandwidth bottlenecks
• Semiconductor manufacturing
• Defense autonomy
• Smart infrastructure
• Industrial robotics
This earnings call changed the narrative.
Key points the market is missing:
→ Nikon commercial deployment is LIVE
→ Shipping 1,000+ industrial sensors already
→ Semiconductor equipment customers using AEVA sensors in fabs
→ NVIDIA DRIVE Hyperion reference LiDAR outside China
→ Defense expansion with Forterra
→ Data center optical interconnect commentary now entering the story
The important part:
AEVA’s core competency isn’t “car LiDAR.”
It’s high-performance silicon photonics + FMCW sensing.
That has applications FAR beyond automotive.

The AI capex cycle is creating massive demand for:
• optical transport
• photonics
• machine perception
• edge AI sensing

And AEVA is now sitting at the intersection of all four.

Still early.
Still speculative.
But this is evolving from a niche EV sensor play into a broader Physical AI + AI photonics platform story.
That’s where rerating potential comes from.

text:
Update on tonight's $COHR print:

Revenue: +20.5% YoY, beat by 1.7%
EPS: $1.41 (missed $1.43 by $0.02)
Q4 guide: $1.98B mid (in-line)
After-hours: -9%

That's THREE photonics names in 48 hours that beat and sold off:
$LITE -8%
$OUST -11%
$COHR -9%

Multiple compression isn't just on premium names anymore. It's the whole sector.

My framework update:
Base case shifted from 3-7 day pullback (was 45% prob) to 8-14 days (now 45% prob).

The thesis isn't broken. The trade is just exhausted at these multiples.

$AAOI tomorrow now needs more than a beat. Needs a SIGNIFICANT guide raise above $1.1B to spark relief.

NFA. DYOR.

text:
Massive YTD so far for PHOTONICS: The next layer of the AI buildout.
AI isn't just a compute story anymore.
It's becoming a data movement problem.
And the market is starting to price that in.
Why photonics is moving
More GPUs → more nodes → more data moving between them.
Copper can't keep up. Too much heat, too much power, wrong distances.
Photonics is the fix. And the buildout is already underway.
What the data is showing
$AAOI revenue grew 34% YoY last quarter — not speculation, actual orders.
800G and 1.6T optical capacity is being expanded right now to meet hyperscaler demand.
Earnings this week are the tell
$LITE — May 5 (after close)
$COHR — May 6 (after close)
$AAOI — May 7
If guidance on 800G/1.6T demand holds or accelerates, this thesis gets legs into 2027.
If hyperscaler commentary disappoints — the chart above gets a reality check.
Watch the guidance, that will be the clue. 
#photonics

text:
🚀 $SIVE Price Targets: Week / Month / Year (Top 3 Catalysts Driving It)

$SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors) has absolutely exploded — up ~815% YTD and trading at ~38 SEK after hitting a 52-week high of 44.70 SEK. This photonics/laser play (wireless, optical comms, LiDAR, CPO for AI data centers) is riding serious momentum. Here’s my take on realistic price targets based on the top 3 near-term catalysts:

1. Nasdaq New York Dual Listing (Biggest Near-Term Catalyst)
•  Announced April 16, 2026 — they’re doing the PCAOB audit uplift and delayed the 2025 annual report to May 15 to prep for it (while staying domiciled in Sweden).
•  This opens the floodgates to US tech capital, higher liquidity, and a potential valuation rerating (US multiples love photonics/AI plays).
•  Impact: Listing progress/news could be the spark.

2. Major LiDAR Ramp (Q4 2026 Production Start)
•  Strategic customer integrating Sivers’ lasers + optical amplifiers across multiple platforms.
•  Revenue potential: $53M–$138M cumulative over the product lifecycle (auto + industrial). Ramp begins Q4 2026.
•  This is real, high-volume revenue coming online.

3. CPO / AI Data Center Supercycle (Longer-Term Rocket Fuel)
•  Sivers is a leader in external laser sources for Co-Packaged Optics (CPO).
•  Recent Jabil collab on 1.6T LRO transceivers for AI data centers + 64% pipeline growth to $453M. Tied directly into the NVDA/AVGO optical boom.
My speculative price targets (NFA — this thing is volatile as hell, high P/S, execution risk):

•  Next Week: 40–45 SEK (Momentum carry + any listing/earnings buzz. Watch for pullbacks after the recent spike.)

•  Next Month: 50–65 SEK (Dual-listing updates + continued AI/photonics hype. Could test new highs if volume stays strong.)

•  Next Year (End 2026 / into 2027): 90–130+ SEK (Dual listing complete + LiDAR ramp kicking off + CPO orders scaling. Market cap could easily 2–3x from here on execution.)

This is a high-conviction, high-risk name — asymmetric upside if the catalysts hit, but it’s already priced in a ton of optimism. Fundamentals are still catching up (still loss-making), but the narrative is strong in the AI/optics supercycle.

Position size accordingly and DYOR. What’s your $SIVE target? 

Holding long or swinging the volatility? 🔥

(Prices in SEK. Current ~38 as of late April close. Not financial advice.)

text:
Never ever FOMO. If you look hard enough, there are always new opportunities to be found.

Glass, photonics, CPUs, NeoClouds, power systems, memory, and everything semis that touches the sun will go up.

If the consensus now is that memory is simply safe to invest in and is the most commonly agreed-upon bottleneck, then the market will do the opposite in the short term, creating a new opportunity, most likely on macro bad news.

Money rewards those who are early and right (i.e. glass substrate) or patient and just on time (i.e. memory).

text:
Optical transceiver and silicon photonics packaging is the actual chokepoint in the AI infrastructure stack that the index trade misses.

Each AI cluster needs hundreds of thousands of high-bandwidth optical interconnects between racks, and packaging capacity is concentrated in a handful of Taiwanese names with multi-year qualified-vendor lead times.

The pricing power lives one step downstream of cell production where Foxconn-backed assemblers sit.

text:
$SIVE old news still very bullish 

Photonics AI interconnects target to $1.1 billion by 2028

Volume production expected to ramp by 2027 https://t.co/QDBFzmdrE6

The 10x Thesis: Identifying the Bottlenecks of the Next Decade $LWLG $SIVE $ALRIB $LPK $INFQ

This portfolio targets the "Picks and Shovels" of the fourth industrial revolution: AI infrastructure, Silicon Photonics, and next-gen Semiconductors. 

These companies aren't just market participants; they provide the critical hardware and software bridges that industry giants must cross to succeed.

1. $LWLG (Lightwave Logic)

The Bottleneck: Data centers are hitting a "speed and heat wall." Traditional silicon modulators are too slow and consume too much power for future AI needs.

The Edge: Their organic polymers enable data transmission at speeds exceeding 800G and 1.6T with significantly lower power. If this becomes the industry standard, LWLG replaces legacy photonics.

2. $SIVE (Sivers Semiconductors)

The Bottleneck: Massive AI clusters require seamless optical chip-to-chip communication, but laser sources are difficult to integrate.

The Edge: Their CW-WDM (Continuous Wave Wavelength Division Multiplexing) lasers are the "engine" for optical computing. 

3. $ALRIB (Riber SA)

The Bottleneck: You cannot build advanced compound semiconductors (like those for 5G or LiDAR) without atom-by-atom precision.

The Edge: Riber is the world leader in Molecular Beam Epitaxy (MBE) machines. They are the gatekeepers; without their hardware, the manufacturers of specialized chips cannot exist.

4. $LPK (LPKF Laser &Electronics SE)

The Bottleneck: Organic chip substrates melt under the heat of high-end AI processors. The industry is pivoting to glass.

The Edge: Their LIDE (Laser Induced Deep Etching) technology is the only proven method to process glass-core substrates at scale. LPKF owns the key to the next generation of chip packaging.

5. $INFQ (Infleqtion)

The Bottleneck: Classical supercomputers cannot solve complex quantum physics or advanced encryption.

The Edge: Focusing on "Cold Atom" technology, they provide the hardware and software stack to make quantum computing commercial. They are building the infrastructure for a post-classical computing world.

6. $SPIR (Spire Global)

The Bottleneck: Real-time global data (weather, maritime, aviation) is often fragmented or unavailable in remote areas.

The Edge: Spire operates one of the largest Nano-Satellite constellations. They offer "Space-as-a-Service," providing high-margin, recurring data subscriptions for the global economy.

7. $WOLF (Wolfspeed)

The Bottleneck: Traditional silicon chips are inefficient for high-power applications like EV drivetrains and fast chargers.

The Edge: The global leader in Silicon Carbide (SiC). SiC chips increase EV range and reduce charging times. Wolfspeed owns the essential material for the global transition to electricity.

8. $OUST (Ouster)

The Bottleneck: For robots and autonomous vehicles to operate safely, they need to "see" in 3D with high resolutio, cameras alone aren't enough.

The Edge: By moving from analog to Digital LiDAR, Ouster has simplified the hardware into a single chip.
This makes their sensors cheaper, more reliable, and easier to integrate into "Physical AI."

9. $SVCO (Silvaco) 

The Bottleneck: Designing next-gen chips (Power, Photonics, Atomic-scale) requires specialized simulation software that standard tools can't provide.

The Edge: Silvaco provides TCAD (Technology Computer-Aided Design) software. They are a "bottleneck within a bottleneck", engineers at companies like $TSM or $NXP use it to design chips that drive the portfolio. 

10. $MRAM (Everspin Technologies)

The Bottleneck: Standard RAM is "volatile", it loses all data during a power failure, which is a disaster for industrial AI and data centers.

The Edge: They are the only commercial provider of MRAM (Magnetoresistive RAM). It is as fast as RAM but keeps data without power. It is the "forever memory" for critical AI infrastructure.

The 10x Goal: While these companies have smaller market caps, they hold the IP and market position required by giants like NVIDIA, Tesla, or Amazon. If even one of these "bottleneck" technologies becomes the global standard, the upside is exponential.

What's your favorite stock?

Not financial advice.

text:
THE SUPER CYCLE ISN’T COMING — IT’S ALREADY HERE.
Most people are still stuck in Phase 1.
Smart money is already rotating.

Phase 1 (Already ran):
Semiconductors — $NVDA $ARM $AMD $AVGO $INTC

Then it moved to:Memory — $MU $WDC $STX  $SNDK
Photonics / Optics — $NOK $LITE $COHR $AAOI $GLW $CIEN $MRVL
Right now:
Compute / AI Data Centers
$IREN  $CIFR $WULF $CORZ $NBIS $CRWV $P

Next wave (most will be late):

Rare Earths — $MP $USAR $UUUU $FCX $AA
Networking — $ANET $AVGO $MRVL $CSCO
Power / Grid / Cooling — $VRT $ETN $GEV $CEG $SMR $OKLO
After that? It gets bigger:
Space — $ASTS $RKLB $LUNR $PL
Defense / Drones — $KTOS $AVAV $ONDS $LMT

Robotics / Autonomy — $TSLA $PATH $SYM $SERV

The pattern is simple:
Infrastructure → Expansion → Domination

If you're waiting for headlines…
you're already behind.

$COHR Q3 FY2026 EARNINGS AFTER-HOURS BEAT – PHOTONICS POWERHOUSE DELIVERS AGAIN! 

Coherent just posted another clean beat and strong growth in its Q3 fiscal 2026 report! Key Numbers: EPS: $1.41 vs $1.39 expected → Beat by 1%
Revenue: $1.806B vs $1.78B expected → Beat by 1%

Growth Highlights: EPS +55% YoY
Revenue +21% YoY
EPS +9% QoQ
Revenue +7% QoQ

Coherent continues to ride the AI/datacenter and advanced manufacturing wave with robust demand for its lasers, optics, and photonics solutions. The double beat shows solid execution and momentum across industrial, communications, and semiconductor markets. Slight misses on the margin of expectations, but the year-over-year acceleration remains impressive. After-hours reaction will hinge on Q4 guidance and management commentary (call at 4:30 PM ET).Photonics and AI infrastructure bulls — another quarter of growth from a key enabler!

text:
Long list but concentration varies wildly across these categories. 
CW lasers for silicon photonics have maybe 3-4 companies that can deliver at volume. Passive optical components have dozens of interchangeable suppliers. Goldman's report lists them all the same. The investment case is very different depending on which layer actually has pricing power.

text:
You guys know you can invest in the stock market without Serenity’s approval, right?

He already mapped out the hottest segment of 2026-2030 (Photonics), the sub-segments (lasers, packaging, testing, substrates…) and even the adjacent beneficiaries, yet it is never enough.

You will grow as retail investor if you follow your own DD and learn from it, even if it doesn’t play out. 

$SMOP, $NOK, $MXL, $M7U, $TRT, $ASOL, $LWLG and hundreds of others are doing just extraordinary.

Just go with the flow of AI / Photonics / Memory / NeoClouds… you can hardly miss with a few exceptions (I’m looking at you, $POET).

Most if not all will continue to go up – maybe not $SIVE up 500% in a month, but 100% in a 1-3 months, which is incredible in and of itself.

Si la demande en bande passante pour les data centers AI continue d’exploser, les composants optiques restent importants. 2. $MXL - Petit semi avec exposition data centers, connectivité et possible optionalité AI infrastructure. 3. $VECO - Exposition aux équipements semi, surtout autour des matériaux avancés, compound semis et photonics manufacturing. 4. $AXTI - Play upstream photonics/materials. Les substrats peuvent devenir un bottleneck si la demande optique continue d’augmenter. Un des meilleurs long de l'année. 5. $IQE - Un des fournisseurs occidentaux d’epi-wafers les plus intéressants. Très risqué, mais potentiellement critique plus upstream dans les supply chains photonics. 6. $RPI - Raspberry Pi pourrait bénéficier si les agents AI locaux, OpenClaw/Picoclaw et les déploiements edge low-cost deviennent un vrai moteur de demande. Au vue de l'augmentation du compute dut à l'agentic et l'explosion des Mac Mini, play ultra smart en AI Local. 7. $EXA- Drones sous marins, souveraineté FR sous la mer. EXAIL a été largement repriced récemment mais reste un narratif de dingue. 8. $ALKAL - Kalray est intéressant comme play européen sur edge AI / data processing infrastructure. Très spéculatif, mais cohérent avec le thème AI compute. L'exposition aux DPU et à la souveraineté européenne en fait un acteur inratable. 9. $ALRIB - Riber donne une exposition aux équipements MBE, importants pour compound semis, photonics et matériaux avancés. Deal avec $MSFT potentiel. 10. $AMD - Une des principales alternatives à Nvidia sur GPUs, CPUs et AI compute hyperscaler. L'agentic est entrain re-rate la demande en CPU, ça tombe bien, AMD fait les deux et même des DPU. 11. $SK Hynix - La mémoire reste un des bottlenecks les plus évidents de l’AI infrastructure, surtout avec la demande HBM. 11 bis. $SK Square - Holding coréenne avec une exposition importante à SK Hynix. Pertinent comme manière indirecte de jouer la demande HBM, mémoire AI et le re-rating potentiel de l’écosystème semi coréen. 12. $SNDK - Exposition storage / NAND. Si la création de données liée à l’AI continue d’exploser, le stockage reste structurellement important. 13. $ROBOTIS - Nom coréen exposé à la robotique et à l’automation. Si le thème robotics continue de se diffuser globalement ça pourrait continuer à grimper fort, surtout après l'ouverture aux capitaux US/EU 14. $VPG - Exposition capteurs pour robotique, automation et systèmes industriels. Pas évident, mais potentiellement important si le physical AI se développe. 15. $GOOGL - Gemini, YouTube, TPUs, Waymo, cloud et énorme operating income pour financer l’AI buildout. Pour moi vrai capteur de la rentabilité de l'IA et la plus à même de la rentabiliser. 16. $QBTS - Exposition quantum computing. Très spéculatif, mais fait partie des noms publics surveillés quand le thème quantum revient. 17. $INFQ - Infleqtion donne une exposition quantum via neutral atom computing, sensing et timing. Risqué, mais intéressant comme nouveau nom public du secteur. 18. $STMPA - Exposition européenne aux semiconducteurs via automotive, industrial, power et embedded chips. Retournement haussier poussée par l'exposition à la photonique silicium avec les PIC100. 19. $NVTS - Exposition power semis / GaN. Si AI, EVs, data centers et efficacité énergétique restent des thèmes clés, ce secteur reste intéressant. 20. $NBIS - Nom AI cloud / infrastructure. Risqué, mais potentiellement un des plays non-megacap les plus intéressants sur la demande AI compute. 21. $COHR - Play photonics/optical infrastructure plus établi. Intéressant si les data centers AI continuent d’augmenter leurs besoins en transceivers, lasers et composants optiques. 22. $LITE - Exposition directe à l’optical cycle, notamment via lasers, photonics et potentielle supply chain TPU/hyperscaler. Plus clean que les moonshots upstream décrit précedemment. 23. $2CRSI - Petit play français sur serveurs haute performance / AI infrastructure. Très spéculatif, mais intéressant si la demande européenne pour compute, HPC et data centers continue d’accélérer. Tout n'est pas à acheter aujourd'hui, faites vos recherches."}}}, "rest_id": "2050981295820550448", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "139487", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2051017744418582663", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "1127", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
These 5 Stocks Will Change Your Life by 2030

This will be me and my bro in 2030 living the good life, with the following stocks:

$AAOI: $180 → $1,200 
$SNDK: $1,180 → $7,000 
$KRKNF: $5.45 → $50 
$EQR.AX:  $0.26 → $2.50 
$ASTS: $71 → $700

Photonics. Memory Supercycle. Underwater Defense. Critical Minerals. Space Connectivity.

Every single one sitting at the intersection of the biggest structural shifts of the next decade. Fiber-optic transceivers powering the AI data center build-out, NAND flash becoming the backbone of every AI workload, autonomous underwater systems changing naval warfare, tungsten supply chains that the West is desperate to control, and satellite broadband granting connectivity to billions of people.

These aren't lottery tickets. These are the picks and shovels for the world being built right now.

Hyperscaler contracts. Record backlogs. Critical mineral moats. Government defense orders that compound for years.

The window is open. It won't be forever.

Have patience. Have conviction.

text:
Goldman Sachs called optical networking a $154B market. Most of the coverage focused on transceivers and CPO vendors.

Here is how I have built exposure across six nodes in the stack, each priced differently by the market:

$SOI - Soitec
Photonics-SOI wafers. The dominant substrate layer behind silicon photonics. The market still prices it like a cyclical materials supplier.

$STMPA - STMicroelectronics
PIC chips co-developed with AWS, manufactured on 300mm wafers. Datacenter revenue guided above $500M in 2026 and above $1B in 2027. The chip layer between wafer and light source, largely absent from optical networking coverage.

$COHR - Coherent
Indium phosphide lasers. The light source inside co-packaged optics. InP is not silicon photonics. It is what makes silicon photonics work at scale. Coherent and Lumentum are the two suppliers with credible capacity.

$SIVE - Sivers Semiconductors
RF and laser components for optical transceivers. Small, Swedish.

$MRVL - Marvell Technology
Custom silicon and DSPs for high-speed optical transmission. Nvidia took an equity position. The market is starting to price this differently.

$SMOP - Smartoptics
Optical networking for edge and mid-market deployments. Q1 was misread. The $0.5M one-time cost was a 4,100 sqm expansion in Kista, just outside Stockholm, on a 7-year lease. Structural demand, not operational weakness.

Six positions across the stack.

The $154B narrative sits at the surface layer. The value chain does not.

This Week's Swing Trade Pond. (Leading names in leading themes) !!!

1) Semiconductors and AI hardware.

Semiconductors (Memory/photonics/others):
ARM, CRDO, INTC, LSCC, MCHP, MPWR, MRVL, MU, NXPI, ON, POWI, QCOM, SMTC, SIMO, STM, UMC, VICR, VIAV, WOLF, TSEM, ALGM, DIOD, HIMX, AOSL, AXTI, AMKR, COHR, ICHR, ACMR, CEVA, ONTO, KLAC, KLIC, MKSI, MTSI, PDFS, LITE, SNDK, WDC, SNDK, AAOI, UCTT, VECO, FORM, ACLS, COHU.
AI infrastructure / accelerator / custom compute: ALAB, AMD, AVGO, CRDO, LITE, MRVL, MU, NVDA, ONTO, MPWR, ARM.
Chip-adjacent and electronic components: GLW, JBL, BELFB, VSH, VRT, LFUS, AVT, SNX, ARW, TTMI, STX, PLXS, SANM, SYNA, CGNX, MXL, MTSI, CIEN.

2) Power, grid, and electrification.

Grid / transmission / specialty power: MYRG, MTZ, STRL, MYRG, CECO, WLDN, TLN, VSEC.
Energy storage / decarbonization / electrification: FLNC, EOSE, FCEL, APLD, CORZ, IREN, WULF, NBIS, CLSK, CIFR, HUT, GLXY, RIOT.

3) Industrial construction and infrastructure.

Construction & engineering: FIX, MTZ, STRL, MYRG, WLDN, PWR, AGX.
Aerospace / industrial systems / motion: FTAI, HWM, ROG, VSEC, JBTM, KALU, MTRN, TH, BLBD, BULL, SPIR.
Industrial distribution / services: AVT, SNX, ARW, VCTR, ECVT, RBRK, IRM.

4) Healthcare, biotech, and life sciences.

Biotechnology: ANAB, GRAL, KNSA, NBIX, NVCR, RYTM, SNDX, TGTX, TVTX, URGN, KRYS, PTCT, VCYT, AGL, BEAM, MRNA.
Healthcare services / facilities: ACHC, BTSG, CNC, DVA, PGNY, OMCL, AGL.
Tools / diagnostics / life sciences: WST, AXGN, AXGN, VCYT, BEAM.

5) Internet, software, and cloud.

Interactive media / internet: GOOG, GOOGL, AMZN, ROKU, TWLO, HNGE, PENG, YOU, BAND, FROG, DDOG, AKAM, RNG, FSLY, MDB, S, CRWV, FLY, DOCN, UI.
Cybersecurity / data infrastructure: FTNT, RBRK, AKAM, CRWV, DDOG.
Enterprise software / workflow: FIVN, TDC, RELY, SPHR, INOD, NAVN.

6) Energy, oil services, and commodities.

Oil & gas E&P / services: PTEN, PUMP, NBR, LBRT, AESI, CHRD, DK, CVE, APA, VAL, DHT, GLNG, FRO, INSW, CVI, PARR.
Mining / precious metals / uranium: HMY, IAG, PAAS, AU, BTG, EGO, ERO, ORLA, SSRM, SVM, MUX, MP, NEXA, ASX, CVSA.
Commodity chemicals / materials: LYB, SXT, STLD, NUE, CSTM, KALU, ROG, ESI.

text:
Nvidia just poured $4 billion into photonics companies. That's not a portfolio diversification move. It's a confession.

The photonics bet tells you Nvidia thinks the next bottleneck for AI scaling isn't compute. It's data movement.

GPU clusters scale by passing data between thousands of chips at insane speeds. Copper interconnects are hitting physical limits. The next leap requires light, optical interconnects, fiber inside the chip package, and laser-based data transmission across racks.

Whoever owns the optical layer wins the next AI capex cycle.

The names Jason Luongo flagged are the cleanest pure-plays. $LITE Lumentum makes the lasers, with a $2 billion commitment from Nvidia and a new 240,000 sq ft InP fab in North Carolina. $MRVL Marvell owns the optical DSP layer, the brains that encode and decode data through photonic links. $GLW Corning supplies the fiber and just signed a $6 billion multiyear deal with Meta for data center buildout.

$TSM TSMC is doing co-packaged optics through its COUPE platform. $AAOI Applied Optoelectronics has $124 million in 800G orders and over $200 million in 1.6T volume orders. $SOI Soitec has a 95 percent monopoly on photonics-grade silicon-on-insulator wafers.

The bull case across all six. AI capex isn't slowing. Hyperscalers are signing multi-year contracts. Pure-play photonics names are early in the adoption curve.

The bear case. Photonics is a deeply technical field with multi-year design cycles. Any execution delay or competitive threat hits valuations hard.

Asymmetric framing. Nvidia spent $4 billion of its own balance sheet because the math told them to.

Retail can't out-research Jensen Huang. Following the capex flow is the cleanest way to position behind the same trade.

Watch the photonics basket through 2027.

text:
Everyone is chasing photonics and data center construction stocks. $COHR, $MRVL, $AVGO, $STRL, $PWR have had enormous runs. But the next wave of the AI infrastructure trade is hiding in plain sight. And one company controls the entire domestic supply chain. $MLI 

The AI buildout has moved in distinct waves, and each wave has been telegraphed well before the market priced it.

Wave 1 was chips. $NVDA, $AMD, $TSM — the compute layer that everyone needed first. That trade is mature.

Wave 2 was photonics and interconnects. $COHR, $MRVL, $AVGO, $LITE — as data volumes exploded, the market figured out that moving data between chips and servers was as important as the chips themselves. That re-rating is largely done.

Wave 3 is construction and site development. $STRL, $PWR, $EME  the companies physically building the data centers. $STRL reported earnings this week and went up 50% in a single session after disclosing that over 90% of its backlog is tied to mission-critical infrastructure projects. The market is figuring this one out in real time.

Wave 4 has not been priced. It is the companies that supply the buildout.

The physical reality of an AI data center is different from what most investors picture.

Every GPU cluster needs power delivered to it. Every watt of power generates heat that has to be removed. Every cooling system, every power distribution network, every grid connection runs on copper. There is no synthetic substitute for copper's electrical and thermal conductivity at scale. It is not a component that can be designed around.

AI servers draw four to six times more power than traditional servers. Higher power density requires thicker copper busbars, more extensive wiring, and more robust cooling infrastructure. The copper intensity of a data center scales directly with its compute density and compute density is going in one direction.

The numbers are larger than most people realize.

A modern hyperscale AI data center requires 27 to 33 tonnes of copper per megawatt of installed capacity. Microsoft's Chicago data center used approximately 2,177 tonnes of copper across an 80 MW facility. Next-generation AI campuses can consume up to 50,000 tonnes of copper per site roughly three times the copper footprint of a conventional data center.

Goldman Sachs projects that AI will drive a 165% increase in data center power demand by 2030. S&P Global estimates that data center copper demand will grow from 1.1 million metric tons in 2025 to 2.5 million metric tons by 2040. Macquarie puts annual data center copper consumption at 330,000 to 420,000 tonnes by 2030. Sprott is more aggressive, projecting 1.1 million tonnes annually by 2030 close to 3% of total global supply.

These are not marginal numbers. They represent a structural re-rating of copper demand that has no historical precedent.

The supply side cannot respond.

S&P Global projects a copper supply deficit reaching 10 million metric tons by 2040. Global copper production is expected to peak in 2030 at 33 million metric tons and then decline as ore grades fall and existing mines are depleted. Opening a new copper mine takes 20 to 30 years in developed markets. The U.S. average is closer to 29 years. UBS forecasts a refined copper deficit of more than 400,000 tonnes in 2026 alone as mine disruptions in Chile, Peru, and Indonesia collide with accelerating demand.

The structural constraint here is time. Even with significant capital investment in new mining capacity, the industry cannot meaningfully expand supply before the AI buildout peaks. This is not a commodity cycle. It is a decade-long structural imbalance.

Hyperscalers are price-insensitive buyers and this point is underappreciated.

When Microsoft, Amazon, Google, and Meta are committing hundreds of billions of dollars to AI infrastructure, copper pricing is not a material constraint on their buildout decisions. BMO Capital Markets has noted that data center developers are among the few buyers in the world who cannot easily delay or downsize projects based on metals prices. Inelastic demand meeting a structural supply deficit is a straightforward setup for sustained price appreciation.

COMEX copper averaged $5.80 per pound in Q1 2026, up 26.8% year over year. JPMorgan projects copper reaching $12,500 per tonne in Q2 2026. UBS targets $13,000 per tonne by year-end. The U.S. imposed a 50% tariff on copper imports effective August 2025, directly benefiting domestic manufacturers while compressing the margins of foreign competitors.

One company sits at the center of all of this and almost no one is talking about it.

Mueller Industries is the only vertically integrated manufacturer of copper tube, fittings, brass rod, and forgings in North America. They control the entire supply chain from raw copper to finished product. No other domestic company does this. Their products are physically present in every cooling system, every power distribution network, and every HVAC installation in every data center being built across the country supplied through HVAC OEMs like Carrier and Trane who incorporate Mueller components into their systems before they reach the job site.

The company is 70 years old. It went public in 1991. It manufactures copper fittings. And for those exact reasons, no one in AI infrastructure investing is paying attention to it.

The Q1 2026 financials are exceptional by any measure.

Revenue came in at $1.193 billion, up 19.3% year over year. Operating income grew 51.4% to $312 million. Diluted EPS of $2.16 was up 55.4% and represented the best first quarter in company history. The company ended the period with $1.39 billion in cash and zero debt a current ratio of 5.4x. Management raised the quarterly dividend 40% to $0.35 per share and repurchased $75 million of stock in the quarter alone.

Note that results include a $41.4 million one-time gain on the sale of the Sherwood Valve business. Stripping that out, clean operating income was approximately $271 million still a significant beat driven by copper pricing and underlying volume growth across industrial, electrical, and commercial markets.

The valuation gap is the most straightforward part of the thesis.

Mueller trades at 17.6x trailing earnings. The U.S. Machinery industry average is 27.5x. The peer group average is 27.5x. That is a 36% discount to comparable industrials before applying any premium for AI infrastructure exposure that competitors like Vertiv, Quanta Services, and EMCOR have already received.

The math is simple. At 25x trailing EPS of $7.76, the stock is worth $194, 43% above current levels. At 25x on forward EPS estimates of $9.50 to $10.00, the range is $237 to $250. At the peer average multiple of 27.5x, the bull case approaches $275. None of these scenarios require an AI premium. They simply require the market to stop pricing Mueller like a cyclical commodity manufacturer and start pricing it like a structural industrial compounder.

The reason the re-rating hasn't happened yet is also the reason the opportunity exists.

Vertiv sells directly to Microsoft. Quanta Services builds the sites. Mueller sells copper tube to wholesalers who sell to HVAC OEMs who sell to contractors. That three-step removal makes the AI narrative difficult to pitch in a two-page equity note. Management does not tell the AI story — on earnings calls the CEO talks about operational execution and raw material management. The company does not disclose a data center revenue segment. Investors cannot quantify what they cannot measure, so they do not pay for it.

The February 2026 selloff reinforced the cyclical narrative. When Q4 2025 EPS came in at $1.39 versus the $1.67 consensus driven by a temporary copper pricing trough the stock dropped 13% despite a 40% dividend increase. The market punished MLI as a commodity cyclical, not as a structural growth business. That is precisely the kind of mispricing that creates the entry point.

The comparison to $STRL is instructive.

Sterling Infrastructure was on every AI infrastructure watchlist for months. Analysts knew it was a data center site development company. The thesis was well understood. But the stock did not move until earnings confirmed it — specifically until management disclosed that backlog was up 78% year over year, that the company had secured initial site development work for a large multi-year semiconductor fabrication campus, and that over 90% of its E-Infrastructure backlog was tied to mission-critical projects.

One earnings report. Fifty percent in a single session.

Mueller has the same setup. The thesis is clear. The fundamentals are improving. The catalyst has not arrived yet. When management discloses data center exposure explicitly, or when a major analyst publishes a note reframing the investment case around AI infrastructure copper demand, the multiple gap will compress quickly.

The risks deserve honest treatment.

Copper price volatility is the primary risk. If COMEX copper reverses materially, Mueller's margins compress and the EPS thesis weakens. Goldman Sachs has argued that copper's rally is driven by expectations rather than fundamentals and sees prices potentially capped at $10,000 to $11,000 per tonne near term. The February selloff is a live example of how fast the market punishes MLI when copper softens. The indirect customer relationship cuts both ways — it is the source of the valuation discount and also the reason a catalyst is harder to identify and time. There is no named hyperscaler contract announcement to wait for.

The AI infrastructure trade is sequential, and the market prices each layer after the prior one becomes obvious.

Chips were priced first because they were the most visible constraint. Photonics and interconnects were priced next when it became clear that moving data was as hard as computing it. Construction and site development are being priced now $STRL's move this week is the signal.

Copper is the physical substrate underneath all of it. It goes into the cooling systems, the power distribution, the grid connections, and the fiber conduits. You cannot build the AI infrastructure stack without it. And the only vertically integrated domestic manufacturer of copper products trades at a 36% discount to its industrial peers with $1.39 billion in cash, zero debt, record earnings, and a rising dividend.

The market will connect the dots.

text:
"A diversified portfolio in 2026: 25% neoclouds, 25% CPU plays, 25% international photonics, 25% memory."

This is the cleanest tongue-in-cheek summary of where the AI capex cycle has actually concentrated.

Neoclouds. CoreWeave, Nebius, Lambda. Pure-play GPU infrastructure providers riding the AI compute wave. CoreWeave alone has signed multi-billion dollar contracts with Microsoft and OpenAI. The category didn't exist as a public investable theme three years ago.

CPU plays. Intel ($INTC), AMD, Arm Holdings. The forgotten layer that came roaring back as AI workloads shifted from pure GPU to mixed CPU-GPU architectures. AMD's data center revenue is up triple digits year over year.

International photonics. ASML for EUV lithography, Lumentum and Coherent for optical interconnect, TSMC for foundry. The "photonics" basket is increasingly the entire physical infrastructure that makes silicon work at AI scale.

Memory. Micron ($MU), Samsung, SK Hynix. High-bandwidth memory is the actual bottleneck for AI training, and memory makers have pricing power that hasn't been seen since the 2017-18 cycle.

What this portfolio tells you. Diversification used to mean different sectors. In 2026, "diversified" means different layers of the same AI infrastructure stack.

That's a real concentration risk hiding behind apparent variety.

If AI capex turns or if hyperscaler spending pauses, all four buckets correlate hard to the downside.

The honest read. This portfolio is a bet that AI infrastructure spending continues unabated through 2028 and beyond.

If that thesis holds, it prints. If it doesn't, all four legs of the table fold at the same time.

Diversification across one trade is concentration with extra steps.

Size accordingly.
